metadata.title: Conditional Matrix Flows for Gaussian Graphical Models
organizations: Department of Computer Science, University of Basel, Switzerland

sections.title: Gaussian Graphical Models: Graphical Lasso
content: GGMs providestructural information: Ωij = 0 implies conditional independence of features i and j given all remaining features
Maximum likelihood estimate of Ω is not sparse and for d > n is not unique →penalized likelihoodto encourage sparse Ω
argmaxΩ>0{log det Ω - Tr(1/n SΩ) - λ||Ω||1}
Intractablebecause the objective is highly non-convex

figures.description: GGM and Graphical Lasso visualization showing observation matrix, precision matrix and graphical model

sections.title: Bayesian Graphical Lasso
content: Bayesian approach: p(Ω|S, λ) ∝ p(S|Ω) p(Ω|λ)
Encourage sparsity with double exponential prior (DE)
p(S|Ω) ∝ Wd(n, Ω⁻¹)p(Ω|λ) ∝ Wd(d + 1, λ1d) ∏ DE(ωij|λ) I[Ω > 0]

sections.title: Conditional Matrix Flow for Gaussian Graphical Models
content: Goal:unify benefitsof frequentist and Bayesian frameworks whileovercoming limitations

We propose a general framework forvariational inferencewith matrix-variate Normalizing Flows in GGMs. As a key improvement on previous work, we train with one flow a continuum of sparse regression models jointly for all regularization parameters λ and all lq norms, including non-convexsub-l1 pseudo-norms.

figures.description: Architecture diagram of the Conditional Matrix Flow showing Hypernetwork, Bijections, and Cholesky product steps

sections.title: References
content: N. Meinshausen and P. Bühlmann (2006). "High-dimensional graphs and variable selection with the Lasso". In: Institute of Mathematical Statistics
H. Wang (2012). "Bayesian Graphical Lasso Models and Efficient Posterior Computation." In: Bayesian Anal. 7 (4)
G. Papamakarios et al (2021). "Normalizing Flows for Probabilistic Modeling and Inference." In: Journal of Machine Learning Research 22
